TN5P_ECOLX - Also known as tnp. Experimental evidence at protein level: Swiss-Prot protein existence level 1. Mediates transposition of transposon Tn5 by a 'cut and paste' mechanism. First, the monomeric transposase binds the 19 bp inverted DNA repeats flanking the transposon. Then, dimerization of the DNA- bound transposase creates a synaptic DNA complex. After nicking of the first DNA strand, excision of the transposon proceeds through a series of intermediates. The transposase then mediates the insertion of the transposon at a new site by strand transfer. The activity of the wild- type transposase is very low, and is further inhibited by dimerization with the transposase inhibitor (inh). From Escherichia coli.
